idea-plugin.name: IDE MCP Server
idea-plugin.description:
IDE MCP Server exposes your JetBrains IDE as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so any AI agent can use IntelliJ's code intelligence tools — inspections, refactoring, navigation, build, test, and Git — over a simple HTTP endpoint.

How It Works

The plugin starts an MCP-compatible HTTP server on a configurable port. Point any MCP client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, your own agent) at it and get access to 70+ IDE tools.

Capabilities

  • Code navigation — search symbols, find references, go to declaration, type hierarchy
  • File operations — read, write, create, delete files through IntelliJ's editor buffers
  • Code quality — run inspections, get compilation errors, apply quick-fixes
  • Refactoring — rename, extract method, inline, format code, optimize imports
  • Build & test — trigger builds, run tests, manage run configurations
  • Git — full Git operations through IntelliJ's VCS layer
  • Terminal — run commands, read output, interact with terminal sessions

Configuration

  • Set the HTTP port in Settings → Tools → IDE MCP Server
  • Enable/disable individual tools with checkboxes
  • Start/stop the server from the tool window
  • Copy the MCP client configuration JSON with one click

Privacy

  • All operations executed locally inside your IDE
  • Server binds to localhost by default
  • No telemetry or data collection

This plugin is an independent open-source project.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0 — Initial Release

  • MCP HTTP server with configurable port
  • 70+ IDE tools exposed via MCP protocol
  • Tool enable/disable checkboxes
  • Start/stop from tool window
  • Copy MCP config for Claude, Cursor, etc.
